hq: 210 Warren St. Apt 24C, New York, NY 10282

system_description.overview: Elevate is an AI-powered data management platform for private-equity-backed roll-ups and multi-location operators. It centralizes, cleans, and analyzes data across disparate legacy systems using large-scale data pipelines, AI-driven transformations, and automated quality checks to unify operational and financial data.

products.Data Warehousing & Reporting Infrastruct: Centralized cloud-based data warehouse with pre-modeled operational and financial tables, KPI dashboards, drill-down analytics, and optional SQL access.
products.Migration Services for Acquired Companie: End-to-end data extraction, mapping, cleaning, de-duplication, AI-powered transformation, and load-ready file delivery with 30-day post-migration QA.
products.Ongoing Data Maintenance & Support: Continuous refresh of recurring data feeds (financials, invoices, wages, etc.), onboarding new subsidiaries, data quality maintenance, and ad-hoc support.

regions: Not explicitly named; described as high-availability data centers with multiple AZs
serverless: Lambda (referenced in network diagram as 'Health Lambda' and 'Cron/Worker Lambdas')

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with ACLs; private and public subnets. VPC endpoints for S3 bucket access. Network diagram shows API Gateway, ECS Fargate services, RDS PostgreSQL, Lambda functions, and Secrets Manager all within VPC.
network_architecture.api_gateway: API Gateway (AWS) visible in network diagram
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS employed to identify and protect against threats
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for remote access
network_architecture.tls: TLS for all web-based communication; certificates from established third-party CA
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server for internet access
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> API Gateway -> VPC (private subnets with Lambda, ECS Fargate, RDS PostgreSQL multi-AZ) + public subnets (ALB). S3 buckets accessed via VPC endpoints. Secrets Manager, Cron/Worker Lambdas also in VPC.

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (Lambda functions + ECS Fargate microservices + RDS)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested before promotion

databases: RDS PostgreSQL (Multi-AZ, visible in network diagram)
file_storage: S3 (S3 bucket exports and uploads referenced in network diagram)
data_storage.data_warehouse: Centralized cloud-based warehouse (technology not named specifically; likely Redshift or S3-based given AWS)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (Active Directory referenced; passwords controlled through AWS Password Policy)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; also for source code tool access and email (2FA required)
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS password policy with periodic forced changes, expiry, complexity requirements; accounts locked after failed attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied)
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (referred to as 'source code management tool' with MFA-protected access)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured; merge requests require additional approval before production merge
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used; baseline configs retained as code with rollback capability
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; critical patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tool that generates alerts at predefined thresholds)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for admin activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configs, permissions and roles; logs protected (access to modify logs is restricted)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts for high and critical risk events; tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure, compute, storage, networking (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection on all production servers (auto-scan, weekly definitions update) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; frequency adjusted as needed
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

untestable: CC2.2.3 - No significant personnel changes during engagement period
CC3.2.1 - Cybersecurity insurance could not be tested (no cyber incidents reported during engagement period)
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion could not be tested (no customer terminations during engagement period)
CC7.3.1 - Incident response could not be tested (no security incidents reported during engagement period)
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to data centers handled by AWS (subservice org)
A1.2 - Environmental controls managed by building management/AWS
notable_controls: Quarterly user access reviews including admin-privileged accounts
Bastion host with VPN for all remote access
IDS/IPS via AWS in conjunction with security policy network settings
Content filtering via proxy server for all internet-bound traffic
Anti-virus definitions updated weekly on production servers
Board has empowered Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) that meets semiannually

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ AWS deployment with VPC, segmented subnets, Lambda and ECS Fargate. No multi-region. No CDN mentioned. IaC-like baseline configs. Single cloud provider.

application_architecture.rationale: Modern hybrid Lambda+ECS Fargate pattern with API Gateway, RDS PostgreSQL, and S3. Separate environments, branch protection, code review enforced. Specific languages/frameworks not disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: RDS PostgreSQL multi-AZ, S3 for file storage, daily backups with alerts. Data classification and retention policies in place. Per-tenant segregation confirmed. No named caching layer or data warehouse technology.

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS, firewalls, annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, anti-malware, MFA enforced, content filtering, quarterly access reviews, cybersecurity insurance. No WAF specifically named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments, emergency change process, IaC-like config management, monthly patch management, ECS Fargate container orchestration. CI/CD tool name not disclosed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, annual review and testing, daily backups with multi-AZ, alerts on backup failures. RTO/RPO not disclosed. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS mentioned as a subservice/vendor. No other named vendors. Very high single-cloud concentration.

overall.rationale: Solid SOC 2 Type 2 pass with no exceptions. Modern AWS architecture with good security practices for an early-stage company. Key weaknesses: single-cloud concentration, no multi-region, RTO/RPO not disclosed, limited vendor transparency.

red_flags: Extreme single-vendor concentration: AWS is the only named subservice/technology vendor
Source control, CI/CD tool, and monitoring/APM tools not named - limited transparency
yellow_flags: No CDN mentioned for a data platform serving multiple customers
No SSO mentioned for customer-facing access
WAF not explicitly mentioned despite API Gateway usage
Cybersecurity insurance could not be tested during the audit period (no incidents to verify)
green_flags: Clean SOC 2 Type 2 with zero exceptions across all tested controls
Multi-AZ RDS PostgreSQL with daily backups and failure alerts
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
MFA enforced for administrative and production access
Quarterly access reviews including privileged accounts
Content filtering via proxy for all internet-bound traffic
key_observations: Network diagram reveals a mature AWS architecture: API Gateway -> VPC with private/public subnets -> ECS Fargate + Lambda + RDS PostgreSQL multi-AZ + S3 via VPC endpoints + Secrets Manager
Elevate processes sensitive M&A financial data for PE-backed roll-ups - high data sensitivity context with per-tenant segregation confirmed
The report is notable for its transparency in showing the network diagram with specific AWS service names
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria were explicitly determined not relevant to the system by management
Company operates with no physical office and all-cloud infrastructure - lean, cloud-native operational model
